page: Microsoft Azure E16-4as_v4 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the EASv4 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7452 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.35 GHz. The server is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a 262 GB drive, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between compute power and memory capacity. The server exhibits poor CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics falling into the bottom 10%. Similarly, multi-core Geekbench and Passmark CPU scores are categorized as weak. However, memory subsystem performance is relatively stronger; large-block read and write memory bandwidth tests, along with the Passmark memory score, achieve average ratings. Single-threaded operations, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, also perform at an average level, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor.

Given the high memory density of 128.0 GB RAM paired with only 4 vCPUs, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive parallel CPU processing. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput all rank in the weak category, indicating that high-throughput transactional databases or heavy web serving may face CPU bottlenecks. The instance is best suited for memory-intensive, low-compute tasks, such as caching layers, small-scale in-memory data processing, or lightweight analytical workloads that benefit from a large memory footprint without demanding high multi-core execution speeds.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-4as_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EASv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor running at 3.35 GHz. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores), 128.0 GB of RAM, and 262 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Benchmark results indicate a significant tradeoff: while memory capacity is high, compute performance is limited, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics falling into the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth for large blocks and single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels, but database operations, Redis throughput, and multi-core Geekbench scores remain weak. This configuration is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads with low CPU demands, such as caching layers or memory-bound data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4as_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7452 processor, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 262 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4as_v4 is an AMD-powered virtual machine with 4 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 262 GB of local storage for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine combining a high memory-to-vCPU ratio with dedicated AMD EPYC processing for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7452 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 3.35 GHz clock speed
128.0 GB of RAM providing a high 32.0 GB per core memory allocation
262 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average performance in large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression
Suited for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads like caching layers
